reason: The statement applies because the text describes Moses as a heroic figure performing extraordinary feats such as turning a rod into a serpent, causing the waters of the river to turn into blood, bringing forth frogs, lice, flies, boils, hail, and darkness as plagues upon Egypt, and ultimately leading the Israelites out of Egypt through the Passover event. These feats are indeed exaggerated and demonstrate divine intervention in a larger-than-life manner.
